abf_relation: AMD is a major consumer of ABF substrates through its server CPUs (EPYC) and AI accelerators (Instinct MI300/MI350 series). The MI300X and MI350X use TSMC CoWoS packaging with HBM integration, requiring advanced ABF substrates. EPYC server CPUs also use high-layer-count organic substrates.

business_overview.primary_business: Designs high-performance CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, and adaptive computing solutions. Major competitor to Intel in server CPUs and to NVIDIA in AI accelerators.

cowos_abf_impact.cowos_demand_driver: AMD Instinct MI300X/MI350X accelerators use TSMC CoWoS-S packaging with HBM3/HBM3E, creating significant ABF substrate demand. Growing AI accelerator market share increases CoWoS consumption.
cowos_abf_impact.abf_substrate_consumption: High - MI300X/MI350X modules are large-format with HBM stacks; EPYC CPUs use high-layer substrates
cowos_abf_impact.supply_chain_risk: AMD competes with NVIDIA for CoWoS capacity allocation at TSMC. Substrate supply is a growth limiter for Instinct GPU shipments.

strengths: Strong EPYC server CPU market share gains (25%+ unit share)
Instinct MI350 competitive with NVIDIA H200 on inference workloads
Open software ecosystem (ROCm) attracting enterprise customers
Xilinx acquisition strengthens adaptive computing portfolio
weaknesses: ROCm software ecosystem still behind CUDA in maturity
Smaller AI accelerator market share vs NVIDIA
CoWoS capacity allocation smaller than NVIDIA's
opportunities: Enterprise AI inference market growing rapidly
EPYC continuing to gain server CPU share from Intel
MI400 series (next gen) could close gap with NVIDIA
Edge AI with Ryzen AI and Versal
threats: Intel Xeon 6 (Sierra Forest/Ponte Vecchio) competing for server share

financials.source: AMD official website (corporate page), industry knowledge
financials.note: Exact quarterly figures not retrieved. Revenue and market cap are approximate based on public knowledge.

data_sources.content: Company overview, founded 1969, high performance computing leader
Instinct MI350 series details, CDNA 4 architecture, HBM3E, MXFP4/MXFP6 support
EPYC 9005 series, 192 cores, vs Intel Xeon benchmarks
Recent press releases (dates visible but content limited)

notes: AMD is the second most important CoWoS/ABF customer after NVIDIA. Its Instinct MI series GPU shipments directly impact ABF substrate demand. EPYC server CPU growth also contributes to high-end substrate consumption. Data partially from official website, financials from public knowledge.
